items.comment: He fancies himself a bit of a philosopher king, which isn't far from true really. He is composing this mostly-serious poem as a way of collecting his thoughts, understanding what he is thinking deep down, and commemorating the occasion.
items.text:
As a boy, I peered ahead
into the dazzle of my own becoming,

squinting to see what lay in wait
down one pathway or another.

the post-noon sun calls me forward,
drawing me with warm and lustrous rays.

items.comment: A pause before this last "few hours" part. He's adding this as a coda with a more-flippant attitude, as one might do in Zen. It's a formalized way of not taking one's self too seriously; he has composed many poems with such codas.
items.text:
We've a few hours before evening yet,
or isn't that so!
